behavior_summary: This script implements the keyring item behavior: examining the item shows a short description ('keyring '), and using the keyring allows the player to select a nearby target to operate on (doors, locks, keys, or other special items).

Use flow (U8P_KEYRING::func0119) suspends to receive a selected target (local1), rejects null or out-of-reach targets, then branches by Item::getType and Item::getFrame and inspects Item::getQuality bits and low-byte IDs to determine compatibility between keys and locks.

When a compatible match is found the script toggles an item-quality bit (0x0400) via U8P_KEYRING::func00C8, plays SFX (guarded by isSFXPlaying to avoid replay), and often spawns U8P_KEYRING::func05AE on affected items to update their visual frame via Item::setFrame.

The script supports transferring items into/out-of the keyring (push/pop/touch) in at least one branch, and uses DOOR_NS::func05E4 to find associated door/lock objects when needed so that both linked items can be toggled.
No global game flags are read or written by these functions; the logic uses local variables and per-item quality/frame state to record open/closed or marked/unmarked status.
notable_control_flow: Selection-and-suspend pattern: U8P_KEYRING::func0119 suspends to let the player pick a target and stores it in local1 before proceeding.
Reach guard: Item::canReach is called early to reject far targets and produce the "can't reach" bark when necessary.

Type/quality branching: multiple nested branches examine Item::getType, Item::getFrame and Item::getQuality (including lower-byte IDs) to decide whether to toggle an item, transfer contents, or play an 'already open' message.

Toggle-with-update: when a match is found U8P_KEYRING::func00C8 toggles the 0x0400 quality bit and may trigger spawn of U8P_KEYRING::func05AE to update the item's frame/appearance; playSFX calls are guarded with isSFXPlaying to prevent duplicate sounds.
open_questions: The precise semantic meaning of the 0x0400 quality bit (open vs. marked key vs. used flag) is inferred but not explicitly named — appears to mark toggled/open state.
Exact mapping of frame indices set by U8P_KEYRING::func05AE to visual states (which frame = open/closed) is not documented and is inferred from clamp/increment behavior.
Some special-case branches depend on specific Item::getFrame values (e.g., frame == 3) whose gameplay role is unclear without further asset data.
Which concrete SFX ids correspond to which player-facing sounds (0x71 vs 0x3B) and when each should play in every branch is partially inferred from the disassembly.

main_function.name: U8P_KEYRING::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short examine/description text for the keyring item (simple bark 'keyring ').
entry_conditions: Triggered by the player's 'U8P_KEYRING::look' or examine action on the keyring item (processType 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_KEYRING::look
function_map.role: examine/U8P_KEYRING::look handler that displays the keyring description

function_map.name: U8P_KEYRING::use
function_map.role: entry point for the 'U8P_KEYRING::use' action that delegates to the primary U8P_KEYRING::use handler

function_map.name: U8P_KEYRING::func0119
function_map.role: primary U8P_KEYRING::use-handler: suspend/select target, reach check, type/quality matching, toggle or transfer logic, spawn visual updater

function_map.name: U8P_KEYRING::func00C8
function_map.role: toggle a specific item-quality bit (0x0400), play SFX if appropriate, and write quality back

function_map.name: U8P_KEYRING::func05AE
function_map.role: per-item visual/frame updater spawned on items after state changes (calls Item::setFrame)

function_map.name: Item::getQuality
function_map.role: read item quality flags/metadata used for matching and toggling

function_map.name: Item::setQuality
function_map.role: write updated quality flags to an item

function_map.name: Item::getType
function_map.role: obtain an item's type to branch behavior (door, chest, key, etc.)

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: read an item's frame/state used by special-case branches

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: apply visual/frame change to an item (used by U8P_KEYRING::func05AE)

function_map.name: Item::canReach
function_map.role: check whether the player can reach the selected target (used to guard operations)

function_map.name: DOOR_NS::func05E4
function_map.role: door subsystem helper to find an associated linked item (e.g., paired door/lock)

function_map.name: isSFXPlaying
function_map.role: prevent replaying a SFX before calling playSFX

function_map.name: playSFX
function_map.role: play sound effects for key/lock operations

internal_calls.purpose: Delegate the 'U8P_KEYRING::use' action to the comprehensive U8P_KEYRING::use-handler that performs selection and interaction logic
Toggle the 0x0400 quality bit on a matched item (mark open/closed or similar) after a successful key/lock match
Spawn a per-item visual updater on an item whose state/frame should change after toggling or transfer

external_calls.purpose: Check if the chosen target is reachable; if not, produce "can't reach" and abort
Determine the target's type to select the correct matching/toggle branch (door, lock, key, etc.)
Read quality flags and low-byte IDs to compare keys against locks and decide matches
Locate an associated door/lock object so both linked items can be toggled together when applicable
Transfer items into/out-of the keyring (inventory operations) and mark items as modified in one branch
Check whether a sound effect is currently playing before playing a lock/key sound to avoid overlap
Play appropriate audio feedback (e.g., 0x3B or 0x71) when toggling or using keys
Read current quality flags to determine whether to set or clear the 0x0400 bit
Write the updated quality flags back to the target item after toggling
Play the toggle SFX (0x71) when the quality bit changes, guarded by isSFXPlaying
Apply the computed frame index to update the item's appearance after state change

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the keyring item behavior: examining the item shows a short description ('keyring '), and using the keyring allows the player to select a nearby target to operate on (doors, locks, keys, or other special items).
